title: viburnum farreri
intro: Viburnum farreri, also known as the Korean spice viburnum, is a deciduous shrub native to China and Korea, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Viburnum farreri is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, as well as acting as an antioxidant and anti-aging agent. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and its berries are also used to make jams and other edible products.
